What are you trying to accomplish? Who do you serve? Whose life so you're trying to change because we're not doing that then what's the point of business? What's the work? What do we have to be doing? What's your vision? Where are you trying to go and then let's get back to work and figure out? What's the process? What's the path? What do you got to create to get there? All right, so that's kind of fun. The second reason I think I want to talk about is why does it actually matter? Okay, how many of you guys got into this business initially just because you wanted to make money? And that's okay. That's okay. You guys can tell me No one will admit it like I had a bigger vision. So when I got started It was all about the money. I'm not gonna lie, right? Okay next thing don't sell to broke people Guess what broke people don't have They don't have money guess what you want to have when you're in business money, okay One of the biggest transformations in our company over the last 10 years Was about four years ago. We sell broke people. Guess what happens a million headaches your life will be miserable Okay, they will complain they will whine they're gonna want their money back when Christmas happens 25 of them will ask for their money back We call it the Christmas refund They're like, oh just hold my money for a couple months and then Christmas I want it back so I can buy Christmas presents Okay, it's horrible. So pick markets that people have money It'll change everything your life will become so much easier people that have money don't mind spending money It's awesome. It makes your life so much easier. Okay, seems simple, but it's important next thing natural funnel hacking don't copy people Okay, some people think me literally with funnel hacking and they just copied everything I've seen so many of my products to look like my product Okay, this is not about it's not about copying. Okay? um, all right the next the next rule and this is a big one um Before I ever create A funnel or an offer anything ever the very first thing I want to do is I want to make sure I know exactly where the traffic is Okay, cannot tell you how many times people come to me like hey Russell. Social media what email list kind of like I want to know all those things ahead of time as soon as I say There is traffic the second my offer is done. This is where I will purchase traffic from Then I will go and build my funnel not the other way around Okay, when the biggest mistakes I see over and over and over again, we build a huge funnel and then we're like, hey, we get traffic That should have been the first question out the second question Okay, and so think about that figure out this funnel hacking process like where are other people getting the traffic from find out Do whatever you can to kind of figure out and reverse engineer that because when you find the traffic it becomes easy okay, um I have a partnership with uh with a guy on our team and we're building this really cool survival business and it's cool And and some people say Russell, why are you building survival business? It becomes so much easier Okay, next thing you got to make sure the people you are you are serving and you want to go after are both willing and able to pay This goes back to selling to broke people right a lot of people are willing to give you money Yeah, I'll give you money for that sweet. This is my money. I'm not giving this to you She had to find markets that people are both willing and able to pay if they're not willing and able don't go in that market Okay, a lot of times we mistake those say yeah, these guys are able to buy I'm gonna sell these guys no, but they're they're able but they're not willing They got to be willing and able it's a key and then The last thing in here and we'll talk more about this in the next after lunch But the next piece is figure out like where do you fit in this ecosystem of this market? Okay, every market has an ecosystem There's other people selling things to these customers Right and the worst thing that you can do and a lot of us do this is we come in right? Okay, this is cool that guy's killing that I'm going to sell something very very similar We come in and we start competing with people right the problem is when you start competing with people It like dramatically especially if you're the new guy in town you lose access to this whole market Okay, and we'll talk about this after lunch. I'll talk about the marketing parts of these funnels But if you figure out instead of how to compete you figure out how to compliment So like here's this ecosystem that guy's teaching this that guy's teaching this like here's all these people doing things Where do I fit this ecosystem? Like what do I have this different that's unique that I can bring to the table? Where do you fit in that ecosystem? And if you can bring that and you can plug in suddenly all these people that would have been your competitors Can now and all become your partners Okay, so it's a really big key. There's figuring it out. What makes you different? Where do you fit in this ecosystem that's That sets you apart. Okay, so that's something was finding a hot market. Okay, so after that you've got a hot market Boom, I know the market Russell. That's what I'm going to do. I'm going to sell to these people This is my hot market now. How to make a potato gun? What is your number one question about how to overcome pornography addiction? What is your number one question about how to increase your vertical? What's your number one question about filling the blank? And it happens at different ages for all of us But it's when we start saying can I do more? Can I be more? Is there more? We transition That is my team helped me get ahead And I want you to see how that's phrased Because any entrepreneur who's ever asked the question how do I get ahead far enough or long enough gets to this question? And it's the wrong one How does my team help me get ahead? Is a selfish early management period Where we believe that all the people around us are just there to make money for us You hear this when entrepreneurs are talking they say things like god, I told my team twice already. I can't believe I have to tell them again Ryan or why doesn't everyone you just do what I say? Gosh when I just said that out loud. I remembered saying that It's messy we don't understand that making a contribution to people is how we actually get ahead And when we transition to from how does my team get me ahead? To how do I help my team get ahead? Is where entrepreneurs lives shift And I'll share with you in detail And I'll share with you in just a moment the biographies of several people you respect I want you to see them in the context of this question, but I want you to think about this Every great entrepreneur who built a team Regardless of who they were before started to see the reflection of value in themselves from that team And started to ask the question how do I help them? And by doing that created the next level of contribution for all of them And what you see in great entrepreneurs is we're so driven to help those around us that we go from how do I help my team get ahead? To how do I help my team get even further ahead and contribution becomes a place where we focus every day I want to tell you something about Russell You know Katie and I in 2007 Started selling a product called CDPE Is anybody I know there's at least one so cool. There's nice. There's a few And in a seven-year period we did 70 million dollars about half of it was online 47,000 real estate agents trained and when people would say are you an online marketer? I used to say no Hell no No I Come here and I think like it's okay to say you're an online marketer You know I mean I think Russell's created an environment here where I'm proud to be a part of this And when I look at what he's asking right now you heard it in what he says you saw it in the presentation He's only asking How do I help my team get ahead and then the next question? How do we help others get ahead? How do we start start helping the world make progress? And what's bizarre is that it doesn't seem like it's a big deal Is that it doesn't matter What the motivation? An entrepreneur starts out with is They end up here when they experience success And your success drives this evolution I can't tell you how many entrepreneurs I started with who had the goal of a hundred thousand dollars or a furrari And I can't tell I don't know one that when they finally hit one hundred thousand dollars a year it meant anything And I also don't know one who, when they could afford the Ferrari, they bought it. We change. Success changes us. And we ask this question obsessively until we switch to, how do we help everyone? And I make a pretty simple argument to any government in the world who wants to understand how to truly get ahead. If there was ever a segment of the population that shouldn't be taxed, it's entrepreneurs because we obsessively make as much money as we can until we give it all away. But we all know there's that time in our lives where we say shit like, yeah, no, I learned a lot of lessons, but you're not going to explain it to your kids. And so Gates asked, how do I get ahead? And here's what he did. He went out and sued everyone after he started Microsoft. How many of you remember this? Like, you know, it's so weird. Anyone who's like 20 thinks Bill Gates is a humanitarian that gives out mosquito nets. And I remember when Bill Gates was in front of Congress, basically going like this for two days, remember? When they asked him a question, he would say, yes. Do you feel that it, you know, Microsoft has an 80% monopoly on blah, blah, blah, blah. How do you feel about that? Like we have a monopoly of 80%. Two days. How do I get further ahead? Well, he sued half the states in the country. He sued the U.S. government, and he sued about 11 countries around the world. Microsoft became a battering ram. How many of you remember this? But Gates writes about it. He goes from this, how do I get further ahead obsessively? How does my team get me ahead? I'm going to go sue the entire world. I'm going to break everything. And then he starts seeing this reflection in the people around him. He actually writes about it. Paul Allen makes a bunch of money. He's a good guy. He starts mentoring people like Bill Gates is mentoring people. I was a consultant in the 90s in consumer electronics and computer electronics. I did business with Microsoft. It was an incredible place to go. The energy there was amazing. And they had this badge system where you had a color that allowed you to go on a color of carpet. So you couldn't go on the brown carpet if you didn't have the brown badge. And there was all these colors, and I had all the colors but brown, because I was a pretty important consultant at that point. Not anymore, but at that point. And I didn't have brown, and I said, hey, why don't I have brown? And they said, well, we don't give outside people that badge. You can't get into that area. That's the area where if you're outside, you can't hear Bill yelling. But if you're in there, you can hear what he's yelling in his office. That was Bill Gates. Then he meets Melinda. Most likely gets lucky for the first time. Let's be honest. He starts hanging out with his friend Warren Buffett, and they start saying things like, how do we help the team get ahead? And he starts vesting in people at Microsoft. They start growing the business. He sees so much in them, he starts going and helping and has to leave. And today, the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation is one of the most active charities in the world. He's literally asking, how do we help everyone? Another example, his buddy Warren, Warren Buffett. So Buffett, how do I stop pressure and noise and what is wrong with me? You tell me. Same thing. How do I get ahead? This one's interesting, guys. Really interesting. Buffett isolated, like so many of us. How many of you isolated and read books, right? And that was part of what got you through. So Buffett read everything. Like he really read everything. And he knew he didn't want a big team. He wasn't good with people. So he had to create a system where he could get ahead without having those things. And so he goes out and he buys a company called Berkshire Hathaway. And here's where he is in, how do I get ahead? How do I get further ahead? How does my team help me get ahead? Berkshire Hathaway was a complete disaster. Most people don't know this. Buffett almost lost it. He had to, out of necessity, transition to how do I help my team get ahead? He knew it all. He had to coach the people around him. And if you read his biography, autobiography or biography and you listen to him, he talks about seeing that reflection and value in the people around him. And now Buffett literally is asking, how do we help others get ahead and how do we help everyone? I want you to think about this. At the beginning of his life, he had one rule he would share with anyone. Don't give money away. Am I wrong? I mean, let's be honest, there was a time where he had his Catillion money somewhere. He spent nothing. Buffett goes from don't give money away to controlling the single largest source of private wealth in the history of humanity. The Billionaires Fund. There's over a half trillion dollars committed to this fund. That is greater than the wealth of many countries. And Buffett, with the same scale that he built, Berkshire Hathaway, that blew past everything, he now has the largest charity in history. Does this make sense to you? This is how we evolve. This is who we are. And here's how you get there. I've already mentioned it a few times, but people like us, we have to be careful. We call this the Contribution Equation. And I share with entrepreneurs that I believe this isn't the meaning of life. And I'm not being sarcastic. And here's where it comes from. It comes from obsessive reading about success and trying to get it down to the basics where anyone could understand it, including me. It comes from reading theology like crazy, like every single one, and saying, what are the commonalities, and how do we make them apply to everyone? And it comes from obsessively working with entrepreneurs side by side and wanting to coach them through any situation in a way that they could move forward. It starts with pressure and noise. People like us have to be concerned with it. We have to know that it's there. We have to understand it, and we have to lower pressure and noise constantly. You have to be obsessive about this. We have to ask for protection and support. I call this the Entrepreneurs' Dilemma. Entrepreneurs need far more protection and support than the average person to get to our full potential, yet any request for protection and support makes us feel vulnerable and exposed. There? So we suck at it. And we don't ask for protection and support. And we're not transparent with who we are and what we need. And if you increase protection and support, things change. Because if you do these two things, lower pressure and noise, increase protection and support, your strengths and abilities will show, though just show up. Watch, let me prove this to you with just a couple of questions. How many of you are using most of what you learned in school to create your success? Cool. Where did it come from? Isn't that a valid question? Where did it come from? How many of you have had the same experience I have had where you're in front of the room or you're asked the question or you're in the situation, and you reply in a way or act in a way that when you're walking away from the situation, you ask yourself, where the hell did that come from? How many of you know you can do that? Those are superpowers, guys. Your strengths and abilities just show. And here's the key. If you do these things, you can make your contribution. And I want to be really clear here. I don't want anyone misunderstanding what I mean by this. Because sometimes people see contribution and they think, oh, he wants everyone to start a charity. I think capitalism is the most pure system ever created in history. And the only thing that screws with it is governments. This is what I want to do the rest of my life. But how do I go from here to where I want to be? And so that's what I'm going to be kind of walking through. So the first concept that is key to understanding this whole thing. This is going to be tied in through the marketing, through a whole bunch of other things. It's a concept that I call the dream 100. And this is not something I invented. One of my friends and mentors, chat homes, wrote a book called the ultimate sales machine. Who has ever read that book? Yeah, good job you guys. I would say one of the top 10 best books for people like us. And in the book, he has a chapter called the dream 100. And when I read this book initially, it had a huge impact on me. He was talking about, at the time he got a job working for Charlie Munger, who was a billionaire, and he was put in charge of this division to sell advertising in magazines. And their magazine was currently like the bottom. Like they had the least revenue of all the magazines in this industry. And Charlie said, chat, you got to get us, you got to increase advertising sales. And so, I was like, okay, well, what am I going to do? How am I going to do this? And so, I started looking at the industry, started looking out there and see like, who are all the players? Who are all the people? Who are the people that actually could give us money? And they looked at all the advertising in their industry, and they found that like 98% of all the ads came from 2% or 3% of the people, right? The biggest companies are spending all this money. So they were focusing on all these other people, all these little people, and they were doing all this stuff, and that's why they weren't successful. Instead of all the people, like this is the group, there's like 100 people or so, that almost all the billions of dollars is coming from. So instead of us focusing on everybody else, just cut those people out, and just focus on these 100 people. And so, Chess set up a campaign where they would get something in the mail, and then two weeks later, you call them on the phone. And two weeks later, they get something in the mail, and two weeks later, you call them on the phone. And he started relentlessly pursuing these people and marketing to them and trying to get their business. I think after six months, he had zero of his Dream 100s had switched over advertising, and within a year, it was like half of them had. And they went from being the lowest revenue magazine to the top. And then he talked about how he used this, and he wrote a screenplay for some movie, and he wanted, you know, he's like, I don't know anyone in Hollywood, I just wrote this really cool screenplay, this would be fun to make it, to get it produced. And he's like, how am I going to do this? And he thought, oh, what if I do that Dream 100 thing? So when he got a Dream 100 list of all the actors, the producers, the directors, people who could actually make his screenplay become a real thing, made a list, and he started this marketing campaign to him, where he would send them something in the mail, then he'd call them on the phone, and he'd send them, and he'd start relentlessly marketing to this group. And within a few months, he had sold the screenplay, and it became a full movie and a whole bunch of other really cool things. And so I'm listening, I'm reading this thing, I'm just like, this is the coolest thing in the world. Like, how, like, it becomes very finite, but I'm like, but for me, my business isn't like, I'm not trying to get 100 customers. Like, my business, that's not how it works, but my business, I know that there are partners, there's at least 100 people in my market who have my Dream customers already on their lists, or on their blogs, or their podcasts, or things like that, right? And so this is probably, man, three or four years ago, we started doing this, and some of you guys are on my Dream 100 list, which has grown now to, I think, Dream 427, I believe. If you're on the list, you get packages from me often. And what we did is we made this list of people, and we started relentlessly marketing to them, trying to build a relationship, trying to become friends with them, trying to get them to promote things, trying, like, all sorts of different things. And so we started marketing to our Dream 100, and it's completely opened up our whole world. And last event, last year, I talked a lot more deep about it. I did a whole presentation on this. So those of you guys who got in early, you have the recordings, I'm gonna go back and watch the Dream 100, because it goes into the marketing behind it, and what we do and how we do it. And we'll talk more about it today, but right now I want you to understand the concept of Dream 100, right? Okay, one of the biggest things you will learn, and one of my biggest ha-has, I remember when I first got started, I had a mentor named Steven Pierce, and I remember I was asking, like, how do I get traffic in my website? I'm trying to figure these things out. And he's like, Russell, you don't have to create traffic. I'm like, yeah, like I need to create traffic and get people in my site. He's like, no, traffic's already there. People are going to websites, right? Facebook has traffic. Blogs have traffic. Traffic's already there. Your job is not to create new traffic. Your job is to be able to identify, like, whoa, there's a whole bunch of traffic coming right here. And then stepping in front of it, boom, and getting that traffic to come to you. And then we start adding them to what we're doing. Okay? So, step number one is figuring out who those people are. Okay? So, now you've got this list of all these amazing people who are already in your market and they're doing amazing things. Next thing you got to do is you got to look at that. Take a step back and say, okay, here's my Dream 100. Whole bunch of people doing a bunch of cool stuff. Where do I fit in the ecosystem here? I don't want to be another me too. Like what do I have that's different? We all have different gifts, right? And there's going to be similarities. Everyone's got different things. But where in this ecosystem of the market that I have chosen do I fit? Okay? One of my friends, Mike Lovic, he taught me this concept a long time ago. I was trying to do this weight loss business when we were really struggling and he asked me, well, where do you fit in the ecosystem? I'm like, what do you mean I'm selling how to lose weight? He's like, yeah, but everyone's selling that. Like where are you different? I was like, I don't know. He's like, you have to look at every market like this little ecosystem. And if you're not careful, you can mess up that ecosystem because all of a sudden you're competing with everybody. Everyone starts hating you and it can destroy your market really quick. Or you come in and you're like, man, I don't want to fight with any of these people. I want to figure out how can I serve them. Like what can my message add to this conversation that's happening in the market? And if you do that, instead of having a whole bunch of competitors you're fighting with, all of a sudden you have a whole bunch of partners and friends and a whole bunch of amazing things can happen. So that's the next thing I'm trying to figure out. I was like, where do I fit in this? Right? Where do I fit in this market? Okay? And that's kind of the first thing. All right. So there's your foundational homework stuff. So now we're going to start moving into these five different funnels. Okay? And here's a picture of them. So the very first funnel, I've nicknamed this the expert making funnel. And I did it, I call this for a few reasons and I think it's, and this is the reason why I want to lead with this funnel. It's probably backwards from a lot of what you would think I would say. But this funnel is amazing for a few reasons. First off, it's going to increase your teachability. Okay? One of the biggest problems we have a lot of times is how many of you guys have ever read a book on a topic and then you're like, man, I know everything about this thing. Like you feel like you're a genius, right? Because you read a book on it. I think I shared another slide. I'm going to tell a story now because it's important. So I had a chance to meet Howard Berg, the world's fastest reader, one of the coolest guys in the whole world. So this dude, who's there, seen Howard before read a book? It's like, if you search on Google or on YouTube, Howard Berg, world's fastest reader, you'll see this. He had this one, then he went on Fox News when the new healthcare bill came out. He read the entire healthcare bill in 50 minutes. It's like this, reads the page, reads the page. And it's like the cool, like it's crazy. We flew into our office and we had one of our friends wrote a book and we wanted to like prove that he's legit, right? So we give him the book. He reads the whole thing in like three minutes, puts it down, and then the author quizzed him and he knew every single answer. Like it was the coolest thing I've ever seen. And so I'm talking to Howard and this dude's read like 30,000 books. He knows everything. So I was like, all right, if I know a guy who knows everything, I'm going to ask him some very important questions. So I was like, like, Howard, I'm curious, what's your opinion on God? And he's like, well, he was like, it's funny. You look at most people and they're raised one way or they have one belief thing and whatever they've heard, that's what they believe. That's the truth for them. He's like, for me, he's like, I was really interested. So because of that, I want to find out what everybody believed. Like what do the Muslims believe in the Buddhists and the Mormons and the Catholics and the Protestants? Like, I want all of them to say, so I read like 300 books on religion and it's like, I went from having like a person of like, this is what I think is true to all of a sudden you get this whole picture of something and you see things differently, right? And they started sharing me all these things that were just like the coolest. It was, I wish I recorded that conversation because it was amazing. But teachability is a big thing. When most of us, when we're kids, like my kids, we have five kids right now, they were here this morning, you guys probably saw them. They're like little sponges right now. Everything you say, they're just like, are you serious dad? They get so excited, right? They're very, very teachable. But what happens to most of us is we're going through our lives, we're teachable, we're learning stuff and then something horrible happens. The worst thing that could possibly happen to any of us, we graduate. We get a degree that says congratulations, you know stuff. You're like, sweet, I know stuff, I'm done. You put down the thing and the rest of your life, you teach that crap that you learned in school. It's horrible. We stop learning. Okay, that's in my life, I had stopped learning when I went to the Tony Robbins event. And I was like, no, that's not possible. Nope, not listening. Nope. And finally it wasn't until I changed my mindset and changed it from a no to a what if. Like what if that was true? And some of the stuff you said I didn't believe, I still don't believe, some of the stuff I took about that doesn't make sense to me, but wow, this piece is really valuable. Like that concept changed my life. That changed my, how I think about things, right? And I changed it from a no to a what if. And also I increased my teachability again. All of a sudden, like things were coming to me, I started using them. I could figure things I started to grow again. There's a long period of my life from graduation until about that time that I wasn't growing. Okay, because I thought I knew everything. And so increasing your teachability is key. And when you do the funnel I'm going to talk about, it's going to give you the opportunity to step away from being the guru for a second and have a chance to become the student and become teachable and be able to learn again. And what's going to happen is just like Howard Berg, you're not going to have your one perspective you have today. Because many of you guys have a very limited perspective. It might be good, it might be bad, but you've got one perspective. And when you do this funnel, it's going to give you the ability to have a very multifaceted opinion on the topic. So that's the first thing it's going to do that's awesome. Second thing, it's going to help you to serve at a higher level because you are going to become better. You're going to know more. You're going to be better. You're going to do better because this process I'm going to take you through will help you to serve people to a higher level. The next thing it does is it builds your platform. One of the hardest things we have when we launch a guru-based business is I don't have a platform. Who made us sell this stuff too? I'm just talking to the wall. We were talking last night about people that are blogging. They're blogging all day and nobody's reading it. Is that really blogging? And Nicole said it sounds more like online journaling. If no one's listening, we need to build a platform. So this process will help you to build a platform. Next thing is going to help you to figure out your positioning in the market. Where do you fit in? What's your unique ability? Remember the prolific index? You're going to figure out where is your sweet spot? Where's the spot where it's going to be? Your message is the right message for your market. It's going to help you to start learning your voice. Because a lot of us, our voice isn't right. We've got to learn that. You're going to learn your voice and it's going to open up all the gates to the future traffic you're going to want for your business and a whole bunch more. They sell you guys on this first funnel? So the type of funnel, the expert maker funnel, is basically what a lot of you guys probably heard before, is a summit funnel. And they are very, very powerful. So basically the way that the summit funnel works is you go back to your Dream 100. You find all the people that are on your Dream 100. You try to make a list of people who you think would do an awesome job sharing their information. Then you take them to a page where people register for a free tele-summit, they register there, and typically we got some kind of self-liquidating offer. We usually sell the transcripts or things like that just to try to make our money back on the ad cost. So that's kind of a real quick preview of what it looks like. Let me dig a lot deeper. Okay. Now we do this through a certain kind of funnel that I call the invisible funnel. How many of you guys have ever heard me talk about the invisible funnel before? Sweet. So this second funnel is a really unique one. And kind of the way it came about, one of my close friends, his name is Dagen Smith. He was going to be here this weekend. We had hung out last weekend at the NCAA wrestling tournament and I had a great time and we were talking about this stuff. But if you rewind, probably five or six years ago, we were just brainstorming ideas and funnels and things like that. And one day we were at an event and we were eating lunch outside the event and we got done eating, we jumped up and we were both ran into the hotel room. We sat down next to each other and about five minutes in the presentation, he's like, Hey man, thanks for paying. I'm like, what? I thought you paid. He's like, no, I didn't pay. I thought you paid. I'm like, oh crap. We didn't pay. So we both jumped up, ran out into the thing and we were like, we're so sorry. We didn't mean to stiff you. I thought he paid. He thought you paid. And the guy was like, oh no, so and so paid. Oh, someone took care of it. But then we got back in the room and we sit down and Dagen sitting there and his head is spinning and he's like sketching stuff on some paper. He's freaking out. I'm like, something weird just happens. I don't know what he's doing, but he's all excited. And then when the next session got done, he leaned over and he's like, isn't that interesting? He's like, you see, we had this amazing lunch. It was a great experience, tasted good. And we felt so much like reciprocation from that we had to both run back out and make sure we paid because we didn't think we had given value back to people. He's like, what if we created a funnel that works that way? He's like, what if we create a funnel? He's like, I don't know how it's going to work. I'm going to try some stuff. So he started, he went to his, you know, back to his house, whatever he's working some stuff and about two, maybe a month, a month and a half, two months later, I see him promote this thing. And I'm watching, I'm watching it from the outside looking in just as like someone who's funnel hacking this process. And he started to promote this thing. He said, I'm doing this live event and this is a premium paid event. It's not free. It costs, I can't remember. I think it was $97. It's $97 to come to the event. But the thing is, I want you to come to the event first and experience it. And I want you to get an amazing result. And if it's awesome, at the end of the event, pay me. But if it sucks, like, don't pay me. Okay. He's like, it's kind of like going to a restaurant and eating all the food at the end of the event. You're like, you know, that wasn't that good. I'm not paying. He's like, or is I going to a movie, I'm sitting through the whole movie and being like, this movie was awesome. I'm going to pay. That movie was horrible. I'm out. He's like, it's kind of like that. This kind of event is going to work. So he's going to come in the way it works. So you got to put your credit card in, but I'm not going to bill you anything. Put your credit card in, come hang out. I'm going to teach and train and serve and give you guys the most amazing result. And at the end of it, if you think it's worth it, let me know. And I'll bill you. If not, let me, that's cool. And we won't bill you a credit card. So he did this promotion and he started doing it. And I'm watching from the outside, just like going crazy. I'm like, this is either going to be amazing or it's going to completely bomb. I'm watching this whole thing. And after a while, I call him. I'm like, what are your numbers? He shared with me. I'm like, this is awesome. So next week, I did my very first invisible funnel. Funny enough, I also called the affiliate bootcamp. And I did this affiliate bootcamp invisible funnel. And I don't remember Dagan's numbers, but I do remember my numbers. So what happened is from this, from this event I promoted, we had 550 people who registered for this invisible funnel event, okay, which is a paper made to put their credit card in. Now, if this would have been a paid event, I was like, Hey, it's a hundred bucks to pay to come on this event. I would have got maybe 30 people. Right. So at 550 that came on for free, they put their credit card in, but they didn't pay anything. They went through this whole thing and then they got on the training and then I had to like, I was on the line. Like I got to deliver. So I started teaching and training. I was on the line for over four hours just serving and giving and trying to blow people's minds because I knew that if I sucked, I wasn't going to get the money at the end of it. So I just did whatever I could. What was interesting is that typically on a regular like free webinar, if you get 20, 25% show up rate, that's pretty good, right? We had 90 or 85% of the people showed up live on a Friday afternoon and stayed on for four and a half hours with me. It was crazy. And at the end, everyone started messing me. I had some people like, I signed up just to like prove you wrong. I was fully expecting to get my money back. Like that was amazing. So when all of a sudden done of the 550 people, we had 10% that messaged me saying, you know, Hey, this wasn't worth it for me. Don't build my card. The other 500 let me build our credit card, which was crazy. And the very end of it, I was like, Hey, by the way, if you guys want some more help on this, we have a done for you options, five grand. And from that, we made a six figures people who just signed up for the five grand thing. It was the most crazy thing in the world. And the end of it, we gave people an amazing result. So that's kind of what the invisible funnel work is. That's how it works. Every January, I do an event with Howard Berg, the world's fastest reader. I do an invisible funnel with him. How many guys have ever been on that event with me? We call it double your reading. All right, cool. Some of you guys. So the way it works with Howard is, and this is the key to an invisible funnel, right? It's all about figuring out a result. You can give somebody a tangible result. So with Howard, I say, look, this is the deal. Howard Berg is the world's fastest reader. He has doubled the reading of millions of people around the world. And I'm curious, would you pay a hundred bucks for someone to double your reading? Who wouldn't write double your reading for a hundred bucks? But this is the deal. Like I don't want you to do it unless it actually works. So sign up for the, for the training right now, jump on. Howard will spend four hours with you, will test your reading, the beginning will test your reading, the end. And if you double your reading, we'll build you a hundred bucks. If not, don't worry about it. You're good to go. Okay. Every Ginary run this is the fun promotion with Howard, but it makes us so much money every single time. Because people come in, they go through his process, at the end, they end with the result. Okay. We had someone in the dating market do this, and they were trying to figure things out. Like the key is the result. Like what's a tangible result? You can give someone. He's like, and the guy was like, well, if you come through my training, I guarantee you'll hook up with a girl this weekend. Like, great. So there's the result. I was like, oh, thank heavens. So I paid $97 CD course was shipped to me. I put it in my car and over the next two weeks I drove around and I listened to the book, thinking, grow rich. And the reality is that people will actually refund because it doesn't end. It keeps going and going. People like finale, like things to end. Okay. The reason I don't do four weeks is cause I have a 30 day refund policy. So I extended my training to six weeks. My refunds dropped to almost nothing. When it was at four weeks, I got a whole bunch of refunds. So there's a reason why I did a six week course and why you should all do six week courses. Okay. There's reasons behind what we're doing. The most powerful question in sales is what made you decide to reach out? You think that's such a simple statement. Why is it powerful? Because I said earlier, humans hate making decisions. They hate it because decisions meant that something's intentional. And if something's intentional, there's consequences that could be good or bad and most people make a bunch of stupid decisions. And so they're scared of having bad consequences, right? Consequences that they don't want. And so when I asked, well, all we're trying to do in sales is get a bunch of, you know, sales is just simply a bunch of smaller agreements, a bunch of smaller decisions of yes, that lead up to the grand finale, which is a close, right? So you're just going to ask them questions and there's some point in the questioning process where you're going to uncover the real pain. People and they think, Oh, my people love me, dude. If they could cut your 30,000 commission dollars out of that in a second, you'd be gone, right? And so what it is, is nobody wants a mortgage. What do they want? A house. But if they want that house, the mortgage is the bridge to get there. So if you're selling a mortgage, you're selling them something they don't want. Nobody wants a 30 year debt around their neck. What they do want is a house and they're willing to suffer that 30 year debt to get to that house. And so if you start selling what they want instead of what they don't want, nobody wants to buy my program because there's 114 videos in it. Like who wants to sit and go through 114 videos? And I walked away without a scratch. And it was a life transformative moment for me. You guys know the rapper 50 Cent. He was shot nine times twice in the face. And he was thinking about it bigger than just about life and spitting in the face of the eyes of fate. It was more like I am devoted to get mine on this planet. I'm going to have my message heard. I finally had a light bulb get finally screwed in on January 29th. And the light bulb told me, God's not done with you yet. That means you need to be louder. You need to be more intense. You need to love all the human beings on this planet with reckless abandon. It doesn't matter if they hate you, you keep loving them. It doesn't matter if they talk shit about you, you don't talk it back. You say, I'm sorry you feel that way. Maybe tomorrow you won't. And somebody says you can't do that and you look at that person and you say, Well, thank you for sharing. And you don't get angry. And you don't shove them back. You don't get militant. Being militant toward people that are your haters and doubters, that's a stage of your evolution. It's better than taking it. But it's not the end all. It's because it will drain your resources. Time, money, and energy will be drained if you try to prove anything to anybody. I don't want you exhausted. I don't want you going broke. I don't want you going emotionally brain dead. And that's going to mean you can't keep living your life to prove anything to anyone. You only need to live your life to improve yourself. That's it. You improve yourself and others will take notice. Get in great shape. Work on your marriage. Work on your own psychology. Work on loving the human race. Work on forgiving yourself. Work on forgiving others. Work on connecting to whatever energetic you think is out there. Work on you. And you will start to create incredible results. And then people, you won't have to tell them, oh, join my program or come by my book. They will come flocking to you because they will go, something's inside that human being, something I want, something inside this human being that I want. And it's invisible and it's magical and it's a flame and it's burning hot and I can feel in the back row. And when they feel that heat from you, you won't have to ask them for anything. They will come begging you for it. And you know what? You won't even care anymore. On January 29th, I had taken my six, five students on a road trip because one of our other students was getting open heart surgery and I've been in a lot of hospitals. And I've had more pain that I wouldn't ever want you to experience. And so I knew when you're sick, when you're in pain, you're worried you're going to die. You don't remember or give a shit about the cards that were sent. You don't care about the flowers. You don't care about the meals that were left on your doorstep. You care about the people that show up in the hospital room and hold your fucking hand. That's who you care about. But do you know how few people are willing to get on airplanes or in cars and come to you? It's small. And on January 29th, I had to make a choice. I had these students and I said, look, I know you all flew here to learn from me today, but we need to pack into a car and we need to drive to Long Beach, California and we need to hug our comrade and then we need to get back in the car and we need to drive home. Because all you have flights and lives back at home and we need to spend this day together to show our friend that we are there for him. And we headed out and I was woken up with that idea four in the morning and it didn't make sense because intuition doesn't use the facts. Not like fear does. And we drove all the way from Scottsdale, Arizona to Long Beach, California and we gave him that hug. He should have seen his face. He didn't believe us. He didn't believe that somebody would take, you know, like a 12 to 14 hour round trip just to give a human being a hug. And he said, who does that? I said, Sean Stevenson does. Because I've been scared. I've smelled the cologne of the Green River. I know when it comes down to the end of it, you only worry about the love you left on the table. And I'm gonna drive home. I did something that I'm not advocating any of you do. It didn't make sense. I took off my seat belt and I turned around to talk to the people behind me. And I was sitting on someone's lap because we didn't have enough seats. But just something inside of me said, just take off your seat belt, turn around, you'll be okay. Now all the while I felt irresponsible because we were on the highway going 75 miles an hour. And as I turned around to talk to one of my students, I noticed they all went pure white. And I can't see anything behind me. And then I hear it. I heard what I thought was just a trash can hitting our hood. And then I watched full grown adults get thrown around in a car like beanie babies. And last thing I knew, I was into my friend that was holding me's body face forward, crushing my nose into her sternum. And I realized we were in a car accident going 75 miles an hour. And you know what ran through my head? What ran through my head was, this was my idea. I'm killing all my students because I listened to something that didn't make any sense. And I got angry. Have you ever followed that intuition, that feeling and didn't make sense? In the beginning you were like, no good deed goes unpunished. This is stupid. And you get angry that you made that decision, that raced through me, and then I dropped that quick. And I went into discussion mode with the magic chocolatey rainbow called God. And I said, God, this was my idea. Keep them safe, take me. I've lived a very good life. I've lived a lot longer than people thought, but do not take them. And like that it was over. And everybody was a mess. And I turned around and I saw that we had smashed into the back of an 18-wheeler and a car was destroyed. Okay, so the second funnel, because some people are like, you know what, bait and switches aren't for me. That doesn't work in my business. I don't have a big promise. You know, I have morals and ethics and that's phenomenal. So funnel two, the trial funnel. The way I see it, I think there's two fundamental trial funnels. There's trial funnels. There are free trials and then there are very, very low cost trials. And I think, at least with local businesses that provide service, which most local businesses do, because products you can buy online, we tend to do way better with a very low cost one for two reasons. One, because people who are doing it are even expecting to spend a little bit of money, means that we're worth a little bit more, right, than free. Even though the lead cost is still lower with free. And then the second thing, and I think this is the main difference, if someone's expecting to spend a little bit of money, we can, I can have my salesman call them on the phone and get them grab their card to hold their spot. And that takes our show up rate from 35% to 85%, which is enormous. And that's what, when Russell said the other day that with the invisible sales funnel, that they have 25% show up for the webinar, but then if they put the card down to then have the $100 spent afterwards, it became 85%. Same numbers, same exact thing, basically. It's just they're showing up for a sale, showing up for something. So I think that those numbers are pretty, pretty accurate and pretty similar for the same reasons. So kind of cool there if you actually have any kind of trials and you struggle with no show rates. So now we're going to get into what I feel is kind of like the capstone of this event for me. I, we've covered a lot of things, right? We've covered a lot of things from personal development things from funnel building and from a whole bunch of stuff. And about, man, six or seven months ago, I wanted to work on a project because it was something that had been going through my head over and over and over again. Like everyone always asked me, Russell, what, like, I don't, like, what's the psychology behind your funnels and why do you do things and how do they all work together? And most of us look at a funnel as like a thing, right? Like, here's my book funnel. Someone comes in, they buy the book. There's the up, sell the down. So that's my funnel, right? And for me, like, initially that's how it was. Like, all my funnels were very separate. Like, they were just different things that were kind of happening randomly at different times. But as I started looking at it, like, not only is a sales funnel a funnel, but like, your entire business, your entire strategy is a bigger funnel. And I started looking back at what we were doing, the things that we were working on that we were working, and I noticed a bigger overarching funnel. And it wasn't just a book funnel or a webinar funnel or a high ticket or a supplement or anything like that. It was a combination of multiple funnels. And it was a process I took customers through. Okay? A lot of times if you offer something to somebody at the wrong time, it doesn't convert, it doesn't sell well, right? I talked about it, I think, two days ago. It's like you've been here for a couple of weeks, right? It's been a long time. A couple of days ago we were talking about, like, if I offer my high ticket thing to somebody, like, it's a hard thing initially a lot of times because they don't know who I am, they haven't provided any value. But if you offer it at the right time, it's a no-brainer. It's an easy sell. And so looking at, like, the progression and how things worked, and one morning I was in my car doing my Mark and Your Car podcast, and I started talking about this concept and kind of explaining it. And I was like, I know, it's kind of like funnel stacking. Like, I'm stacking funnels together to actually build a bigger funnel that happens over a period of time. Not just over, you know, like, you know, five minute time, so come to your web page time, they leave, like, a funnel that overarchs over a month, two months, three months or beyond. And I kind of said that on this podcast, and I got more excited. I'm like, this is really cool. Like, I want to, you know, I kind of had different funnels and things were kind of connected, but there was never, like, a real strategy behind it outside of just like, someone's going to buy this, and eventually somebody will find out about this, and somebody will find out about this, and that's kind of how it was. And so I started coming back to the drawing board and thinking, like, strategically, like, this makes the most sense if I take them through a process, like, what's that process look like, and what's the smartest way to do it, and how does that work? And so I spent the next couple of months kind of building that out and really connecting things and making them just kind of work together as a whole, which was really exciting for me. And then I decided, like, I'm going to create, like, I'm going to write out this whole process. So I spent the next, I don't know, way too long, writing kind of like a book, and I called the book Funnel Stack into three core funnels, and not only the three funnels, but how they all work together. The other interesting thing that I noticed while we were doing this process is that we kind of did a, we took all of our income from all of our funnels, we found out that about 98% of our income came from three core funnels, and it was these three. And so I thought, man, we need to be focusing more here because that's where the majority of our profits have come from. And so that's kind of the title of this presentation. I did create a book that is done, it's in the shipping warehouse, and if you guys, do you guys all get that postcard on your things today about Funnel University? So Funnel University is the continuity program that's going live next week. When you sign up for it, you get a free trial, we ship you a copy of the book. So next week you guys can all get a copy of the book, even if you just sign up and then cancel the next day, we'll still ship you out the book. But that's kind of the game plan, is this kind of like the curriculum for Funnel University. So the book's awesome, but one of your now's, I'm going to go through the strategy and all the numbers and the stats and everything that are inside the book so you guys can understand how they all weave together and what happens first and second and why we do the sequences and how we do them, and it's going to be amazing. And like I said, next week when we do launch this, if you guys get the book, you'll have it and you'll have it all documented and it'll be awesome. So that is the game plan. Alright, so if you look at the three funnels, hopefully this is working, the three core funnels that we looked at were about 98% of our income came from these three funnels, from the tripwire funnel, from webinar funnel, and from a high ticket funnel. If you've read the Dotcom Seekers book, one thing I talk a lot about is traffic temperature, right? We start out with cold traffic, we have warm traffic, and we have hot traffic. Okay, hot traffic, these are people who are obsessed with you, they know you, they love you, pretty much anything you offer them, they're probably going to buy. How many of you guys would pretty much buy anything that I offered you? Thank you, that is awesome. Well, what do you guys want to buy? We could create something right now. I'm up for it, just kidding. So, warm traffic is like the easiest, right? But it's hard to scale a business on warm traffic. So then you move back to warm traffic, and warm traffic is people who are typically in your market, in your industry, like they are aware of what's happening, but they may not know you yet. Okay, so your warm traffic is a little bit different. So typically I consider Facebook warm traffic, right? If I'm targeting Tony Robbins fans, or Garrett's fans, or other people's fans, they may not know who I am, but they understand the market. So I can speak to them in a way and be like, hey, you want funnels? Sweet, you know what a funnel is, come on in. Right, it's really easy. And then the last step is to go out to cold traffic. Cold traffic, people have no idea who you are. Okay, they don't know who you are, they're the people at the mall who are walking around, and they're kind of doing some things. Now, in our company we have a test to find out if the things that we're creating, if they will convert to cold traffic, we call this the food court test. Okay, so imagine yourself, you walk into the food court at the mall, and you see all these people, right, they're eating at McDonald's, and KFC, and Panda Express, and they're all eating this food, right? So you walk in the food court, you look around, and you're trying to see if anyone is going to want my product. So imagine pulling out a stool and standing up on it, and top of your lungs screaming out, who here wants to learn how to make funnels? Now, if anybody in the food court stands up, that means you've got an offer that will convert to cold traffic. Okay, how many people think that if I yelled out about funnels in the food court, anybody would even pay attention to what I was talking about? They probably think I want funnel cakes, right? They have no idea. And so the problem is a lot of you guys are creating offers, you're creating things with verbs that your warm audience will understand, but cold traffic will never understand, just because the words you use are wrong. Okay, so the food court test is, for example, say, walking the food court stand on chair and say, who here wants to get my free book about how to make money on the internet? A lot of people in the food court are like, yeah, I want a free book. I want to make money on the internet. It's different. So that's kind of how hot to warm to cold traffic works, right? Now, ideally, when we start scaling a business, we want to go after cold traffic, and someone comes in, and then we move them from being a cold person to through the process of them buying and consuming what we are selling, they start warming up to us, they become warm, and eventually they became hot. So my mind is how things kind of work, okay? If you think back about how you guys got introduced to ClickFunnels, all of you guys started a different path, right? Some of you guys came in, you were a cold audience, and we had to convince you that this funnel thing was actually really cool, right? Some of you guys were like, I love funnels, but I use this crappy thing called lead pages all the time, and we're gluing together pages. It's horrible, right? And I was like, we got a better mouse trap, and you're like, sweet, I'm in. And some of you guys have been following me for years, and you're like, Russell's got a new thing, I'm in, and it was easy, right? And so think about, like, in your journey, you guys all came at different things, and the messaging was different at each of these steps, because you guys wouldn't have responded to different things. Does that make sense? And so for me, I'm looking at these three funnels, and I'm taking somebody typically from cold to warm to hot through this process. So those are the three funnels. So I'm going to break down each of these three funnels that we use, show you guys some examples, and then how we kind of glue these three things together when we do our funnel stacking. So funnel number one, we call a tripwire funnel. Now, the one I'm going to, the example I'm going to be showing you guys is the dot com secrets book funnel. And so we had, what's that, a 14% conversion rate from traffic to actual credit cards. Okay? So those are some numbers. Am I doing, like, am I close? Am I wrong? Where am I at? So this I want to show you guys is like, this is kind of the framework. Like, if you can get your conversions close to that, you're probably doing right. If they're lower, you should probably fix some things. If they're higher, you should call me and tell me what you did so I can copy you. I mean, funnel hack you. Okay? Because I want to know. But this is like a baseline. Like, this is kind of where the numbers could and should actually be at. Okay? So that's number one is the actual free offer. All right? So number two now is the order form bump. Now, this is something that we discovered this, man, probably eight or nine years ago. Back in the day, we had, before ClickFunnels, back when we were hand coding all of our pages and our shopping carts, there was a nightmare. We had this thing that we were selling, and it was a product, and we had a physical version and a digital version. And I'm the kind of person that loves physical things. How many of you guys love buying physical products, books and CDs and DVDs, right? How many of you guys would rather have the digital, so you can put it on your Kindle or your iPhone? Yeah. About half and half. Interesting. So for me, I only want physical things. The problem that sucks about physical things is you got to print them, you got to ship them, you got to store them, like all these extra costs. And so I was like, what I'm going to do is I'm going to make this offer where it's like, it's basically the digital version for like, I can't remember what it was, $97. But if someone doesn't want the actual physical version, like me, like I would pay an extra, whatever, $500, you know, whatever that is for the physical, I would pay that because I would rather have that. So we made this little thing and at the time we called it an order form bump. Like let's make this bump thing where it just says, hey, if you want this thing, click on this button here and we'll add a physical version to your order. So we added this on the order form. And again, this was seven or eight years ago. We started doing traffic through it. And we moved this thing around. Initially we had it at the very top. And it didn't convert very well. Oh, it did horrible. I'm like, oh, but I like, conceptually I like it. I'm like, well, let's move it. So we moved it around. And eventually we moved it between where the credit card information happens and above the order button. And as soon as we did that, it just, it was like magic. People came, they first needed to put in their credit card so they've already made a commitment. And then they see this offer. It's like, oh, do you want to add this thing? Oh yeah, sure, I'll do that. Do you want to add fries and a Coke, right? Yes, I'll do that. It's like a no brainer offer and you click on it. And when we first started doing that, it was about 65% of the people were taking that bump, which dramatically increased our revenues. Right now, we probably ran this campaign for ourselves or for friends and things like that, at least 20 different times. And right now our conversion rate on this style, right, I'm next page, sorry. I forget, I can see the next slides and you guys can't. So the conversion rates on this little order form bump right now, on the low end, about 22% on the high end, about 47% or more. Like that's kind of where it's at. And this is just like free money, especially like a free plus shipping offer. It's kind of scary because like you're losing money on every offer, right? This order form bump typically recoups all of your costs. If you're getting one out of three people to buy that, it's equivalent of selling, like, you know, selling a product. So you make your money back a little bit quick. I have a testimony. It was Dean in the room. I think I saw Dean. Okay, I just pushed your screenshot from Facebook. Okay, when you get, and I haven't talked a lot about this, but like one of our goals in our igniting circle programs is like, you need to create a break-even funnel where you can break even. Because if you can break even, like people all the time, like, oh, Ruff, so I ran the webinar, I didn't make any money. I'm like, you didn't? Did you lose money? No, like I broke even. Like, dude, that's a huge success. You broke even. You got a customer for free. Like, that's the whole goal. If you have a break-even funnel, that's amazing. Now we spend about $12 on Facebook for one of our free-plus shipping offers. Like, right now, before they've even passed that, I've already more than broke even. Anything else that happens from this point forward to that customer is free money. Okay, it's just, it's one of the most amazing things. Like, the order form bumps, you guys aren't using them. Like, you should start using them. Next, we'll make you free money all day long. Alright, next thing. Now, Upsell number one. So after they bought the free book, now we got to offer them the next thing. Now, this is the mistake that I made over and over and over again as I've critiqued some of your guys' businesses. Something that consistently happens. The problem that we have is we offer something amazing, right? And then your Upsell is more of that same thing. Okay, logically it makes sense. The first time I learned this, we had our split testing book. 108 Proven Split Test Winners. We were selling that thing. I thought, man, people who buy a book about split testing, they must love split testing, right? So many of you said, I'm going to have an Upsell for $200, and I'm going to get all these split testing interviews and audios and courses and software and all this split testing stuff. Like, logically it makes sense, right? This guy's interested in split testing. He should buy more split testing. Okay, we ran that offer. I rebuilt the funnel seven times, could not get that offer to convert. We wrote the script, we wrote the picture, and I'm like, why is this not working? You told me you want split testing stuff. Why are you not giving me money for more split testing stuff, right? There's this disconnect. And one day I had a friend, his name's Tim Urway, some of you guys may know him. He went through, he bought my book funnel, and they called me up. He said, hey man, I bought your book, super excited. I bet you $50, your Upsell sucks. I'm like, what do you mean? He's like, there's no way it's converting. I'm like, whatever, dude, it's converting. It's awesome. He's like, I guarantee it's not. What are the numbers? Show me the numbers. I was like, all right, man, this is the deal. It's not converting at all. We've rebuilt it seven times. I have no idea why. It's really embarrassing because this is a book about split testing and how we're really going to get it converting things. I can't get the Upsell to convert. Please don't tell anybody because it's really embarrassing. And he laughs. He said, this is the reason, dude. He's like, think about this. Let's say you are a little bit overweight and you want to lose weight. And you see an ad that says, lose weight, get a six pack. You click here and you buy this book about how to get a six pack. Even though you don't have a six pack yet, the second you purchase that thing, you take your credit card and you buy it, in your mind, you're like, sweet, I've lost weight. It's done. I had an itch and it's been scratched like it's taken care of. So if your Upsell is like, hey man, you got the book, it's coming to you. But if you really want to learn how to lose weight, I've got this really cool diet program you can do. And you're like, but I just, I thought, I thought we just took care of that. Like I solved that. I gave you money. I solved that problem. It is gone. Okay. You said, you have to understand this. Soon as someone gives you money in their mind, the problem has been solved. And if you offer them the same thing, they're not going to buy. This was a huge breakthrough for me. And he said, you've got to sell the next thing. He's like, when they purchase this thing, this itch has been scratched. What's the next logical thing that they need? What holds, what things are opened up because they now have this? Okay. As soon as you buy this book about split testing, now you can convert well, but the thing that you need next is you need traffic. So I changed my offer from more split testing to traffic and it exploded. Okay. From weight loss, you're selling the weight loss product. You look at Venus Factor, look at the good ones. They're looking at the next thing. You bought the weight loss thing. Now what do you need? You need exercise plans. You need recipes. You need, what's the next thing you need? So I'm always thinking about this now with my sales processes. What's the next thing that somebody needs? Okay. So for this, for the Dockham Singers Book Launch, this is the upsell page. It's very simple. The video also is very short. Upsells, the other thing you have to understand is your goal with an upsell is not to try to sell somebody on this concept. They already bought into the concept. This is why Free Plus Shipping offers are one of my favorite things in the world because it's, you come to this thing, right? And you're like, oh, there's this book. Like, it gets just free. It's easy. Like, and in your mind, you're like, okay, I buy, I'm buying into this concept enough that I'm going to pull my credit card out. Right? So you got them to say the first yes. First yes is the hardest. Okay. They've already bought into the concept. Like, so the second, the upsell video for me, usually about two or three minutes long and all it's saying is, thank you so much for buying the book. Now, after you've got this book, this is the next thing you need. I don't sell it. I don't pitch it hard. I just kind of tell them, here's the next thing. And then we offer to them at a special price. Okay. And so it's not a, it's not a resell you and tell you a big story about why this is amazing. Everything else is just explaining that now that you've got this, this is the next thing you need. And they're very short and very easy videos. So there's the upsell page. Now some of the numbers on this campaign, the price for this offer was $197. From that way, 28,000 visitors came through. We had 1,274 people who bought it. So about 4% conversion rate. So that's, for me, that's a lower conversion rate, but the dollar amount was higher. And so if you look at the actual numbers from all said and done, we were at $18.31 prior. Total revenue from upsell number one to $506,000. Wait, no, $250,000, sorry. Which basically ended up bringing up our costs or our profit per book up to $27.39. Okay. So that's the numbers. Yeah. OTO ones. We made $250,000 from the first upsell. Does that make sense? Yes. Okay. Sorry. This screen's a little more blurry than it should be. All right. So there it is. Now in this offer, in this funnel, I didn't do a downsell, but this is where I would do a downsell. Downsells are really nice if somebody, if you have a higher price thing and people aren't able to buy it because of the price. And so this is what my downsell pages look like. And basically it's really simple. There's a video of me saying, look, if you didn't just buy that last offer I made you, there's only one of two reasons. Excuse me, there's only one logical reason. That's that you can't afford it. Because this is so good. I know you want it. I know you need it. So if you didn't buy it, it's the only logical reason is you can't afford it right now. So because of that, I'm going to give you a really cool thing. I'm going to give you a payment plan. This is how the payment plan works. And you kind of just take one more shot at it. And we get huge conversion rates typically on these. And I usually give both options. Because for some reason, even though people say no, the first offer, the second offer, they see the payment plan and then they're like, I'll just pay for it up front. So we see, on the second page, we get tons of people who buy both of the different options. So I usually have both options on the downsell page. So that's the downsell page. I don't have numbers because I didn't have one in this campaign. But if any of you guys have an upsell, it's higher ticket. Drop selling payment plan is the best way to go. All right. Because I've seen people, and I hate this, people are like, OK, you said no to this thing at $197. What I'm going to do is I'm going to give you everything, I'm going to take the bonuses away and it'll be $97. And that just makes me feel dirty. I don't like that at all. So I just like offering payment plans on downsells. All right. Now this funnel, we had a second upsell, which again, I came back to the same upsell concept before. What's the next thing somebody needs? They just bought this. What's the next thing they need? OK, so our second upsell for us was our sales training, which looks very similar. Same thing, I think I'm thinking of the thing they just bought. Here's the sales training. Our numbers on that. 24,000 people have this page with 439 purchases, 2% conversion rate. When all is said and done, it made $130,000 from that offer. And now we're up to $32.10 in total profit from every single book that we sold so far in this funnel. Isn't that exciting? When you're making $30 for a book, guess what you can do? You can spend up to $30 on Facebook or on Google or on YouTube or on wherever. You can give affiliates. In fact, when we did this launch initially, we were paying our affiliates $20 for a free book they give away. And we still made money on the front end. It was crazy. And affiliates went nuts for that. And so it's a cool thing that you can do. One of the things that most people that I talked to don't know, I always ask them, what's your average cart value? This metric is the most important for your front-end funnels. Average cart value. What that means is on average, everyone that goes through this, what is the average amount of money you collect? So my average cart value is $30.10. If you don't know the average cart value for your front-end funnels yet, that's a metric you need to understand because that is what dictates how much money you can spend on advertising. Does that make sense? Okay, so if you don't know that number yet, that's the key metric. Go figure that. Go through the last month, six weeks, whatever it is of sales, do some data, figure it out. And basically, I was taking all of your sales that came through all the money collected, dividing by how many numbers of the front-end you sold, and that gives you your average cart value. This is coming from mathematician. You guys impressed? Okay. And so you have the average cart value. Open them to the whole world because now you go to every single source and say, I can spend up to $32.10 to acquire a customer and still be a breakeven. Some of you guys may be kind of risky. Like we eat back in the day, we'd sell free CDs for $4.95 shipping and handling. We had no upsells on the back end or anything, but we were paying $38 for every free CD we sold because we knew after that we'd call people and sell and coach them on the ends. We were spending four or five times as much money to sell these things we were making. So this is, if you get to breakeven, like for me, breakeven is like insane. So I can spend $32 still breakeven and I got a customer. Okay. Until you know your average cart value on your front-end funnel, you're just guessing and you're nervous. Like, I'm going to go, I want to try YouTube ads, but I don't know what I can spend and I'm freaking out and how much did I budget? Like, you don't need to worry about that stuff. Just go and try the ads. If you can acquire a customer for under your average cart value, just run it all day long for as long as you want. Okay. That metric will set you free, you guys. All right. So there you go. Now, I'll add some of the book, then we take it to our offer wall. This is something we learned from some of the, I don't know how I can say it, some of my friends in the less ethical part of the world. There's some, anyway, I'll leave it there. Okay. So the offer wall at the end, I said, thank you so much for buying my book. You guys are amazing. We're going to ship it out to you. Download, download below. You can log in the membership site, things like that. And then underneath that, I just put a whole bunch of links to other stuff I sell. Okay. This is my offer wall. All these things down here. I'm like, oh, you want a free t-shirt? Oh, you want a free thing? Oh, you want a free thing? Because what do I have right now? I've got active buyers. People who've bought something can know something really interesting about buyers. People who buy will continue to buy until one of two things happens. Number one is you offend them. Number two, you quit selling them things. Okay. Like, that's it. I'm a buyer. I keep buying. I am obsessed. I buy everything that I can in the markets I'm excited for. And your people are the same way. They just buy your free book, your free thing to freak it out. And they're like, I get a free, another free book? I get a free webinar script. Like, they will keep buying. So do not take that opportunity away from them. They want to give you money. Okay. If they don't get it from you, then you go to someone else. They're going to get the book. They'll be done. They're going to be bored out of their mind. They'll be back on Facebook looking for something else to buy. And your competitors will get their money. So you have an offer wall. I didn't track the actual metrics from this. I do know that during this campaign, we sold, I think, 2,300 of the perfect webinar scripts, about 1,500 of the 108 split test books. And we added about 500 or 600 people to our continuity program. So a lot more revenue that I didn't even track came back to that front of phone just by having an offer wall. Here's other stuff you can buy for me. It's just easy, right? Like, it's not scary. It's just awesome. Okay. There's an offer wall. Some of you guys think, this is cool Russell, but what if I don't have a book? Writing a book is so hard. That's true. Writing a book is really hard for those who have never done it before. So you don't have a book. That's okay. There's other ways you can do this. So for those who don't have a book, the concept I need you guys to understand, if you understand this, it becomes really easy. Okay. This is from chapter one in the dot com secrets book. It's a concept I call the secret formula. The secret formula is this. Okay. The first step in launching a business, you have to figure out who it is that you want to sell things to. Who do you want to attract? Who do you want to be your dream clients? Okay. That's number one. Number two question is where do these people congregate? Where, what Facebook groups are they on? What email lists are they on? What blogs are they reading? Like where do these guys actually congregate? And the third step here is the bait. This is the key here. What's the bait that's going to get those people to raise their hands? Okay. So you have to think about that. So who's your dream client? Where are they at? And what's the bait you're going to use to get them? And the results, the ascension through the funnels is the result. Okay. So the key here is understanding the bait. So for your audience, like what is the thing that gets them excited? Okay. How many of you guys got excited by that funnel graffiti we put in your bag on two days ago? Is that cool? I know that funnel graffiti is bait for people who like to build funnels. Okay. So I built bait based on what I know my audience would like. Okay. That'll become a free plus shipping offer. We're going to go and use that as bait to get people in. My book is bait. Tons of things like that. A couple of examples. People who like gun targets like to buy gun stuff, right? I think, I assume. Okay. We just started filming a documentary last week of his kind of journey. We were out there in offices. And people who buy this are going to buy the suits, they're going to buy the shirts, they're going to buy everything else they have, right? So this is bait for their dream client.
